title: rubus leucandrus
intro: Rubus leucandrus, also known as the white bramble, is a species of thorny shrub in the rose family. The medicinal properties of Rubus leucandrus are attributed to its high antioxidant content, which includes ellagic acid, a compound with potential anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and potentially prevent certain types of cancer. Rubus leucandrus can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and extracts, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
